summary: Obfuscated published bundle without lifecycle hooks or remote fetches
details: The package's main + bin entry (dist/agent-flow.js, ~1MB) is processed with javascript-obfuscator (string-array, hex-renamed identifiers, RC4-style string decoder). No npm lifecycle scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall) are declared; nothing runs automatically on `npm install`. Tracing through the decoded strings shows standard library usage (chalk, debug, ms, http-errors, iconv-lite, express), spawning of the `claude` CLI, reads of `GH_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` consistent with the stated Linear/GitHub Projects kanban purpose, and a local listener — no remote code fetch, no eval of fetched content, no scraping of installer secrets, no exfiltration endpoints. Obfuscation of a public npm bundle is poor hygiene because it impedes consumer auditing, but on its own it is not a supply-chain attack. Routed for human review so a reviewer can confirm the obfuscated bundle's behavior matches the package's advertised purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
